description: A type confusion vulnerability exists in V8's WebAssembly tier-up mechanism within Runtime_TierUpWasmToJSWrapper (src/runtime/runtime-wasm.cc). The bug was introduced by commit 08170169a305fab1dca42bc11d86d7400f25421e, which added support for the text-encoder:encodeStringToUTF8Array well-known import (WellKnownImport::kStringToUtf8Array). Unlike all other well-known imports that use abstract heap types (e.g., externref), this new import's function signature contains a module-specific indexed i8 array reference type as the return value (ref $i8array). During module instantiation, when the import is handled via the generic Wasm-to-JS wrapper (kWasmToJsWrapperAsm), the full module-specific signature including the indexed return type is serialized into WasmApiFunctionRef::sig via SerializedSignatureHelper::SerializeSignature. When the generic wrapper's call budget is exhausted and tier-up is triggered, Runtime_TierUpWasmToJSWrapper deserializes the signature and calls TypeCanonicalizer::AddRecursiveGroup(const FunctionSig*), which strictly requires module-independent signatures with no indexed types (enforced by DCHECK(!type.has_index()) at src/wasm/canonical-types.cc:131). In debug builds, this causes a fatal assertion failure crash. In release builds, the module-specific index is silently accepted as a canonical type index, corrupting the type canonicalization table and potentially enabling type confusion in the Wasm type system.

fixes.evidence_text: 2109613ad4622028778a38fb418956fab8b478b6 [wasm] Use correct signature index for tier-up of wasm-to-js wrapper
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/5291374 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
